settings.title: Our Packaging Work Took a Regional Award
settings.excerpt: A label we designed for a tiny local cidery just won something we genuinely did not expect.
settings.body:
This month a project we are quietly proud of picked up a regional design award, and we want to share the credit where it belongs.

The work was a set of labels for a two person cidery down the road. They gave us a free hand and trusted the strange idea, which is the kind of client that makes good work possible.

How the labels came together

The cidery makes small batches and wanted the bottles to feel handmade without looking amateur. We leaned into that, with an off-kilter layout and a colour that nobody else on the shelf was using. It was a risk, and they let us take it.

  • A hand-set look that matched how the cider is actually made.
  • A shelf colour chosen to stand apart, not blend in.
  • A system simple enough for two people to print and apply.

Awards are nice, but the part that stuck with us was the owner texting a photo of the bottles selling out at a weekend market. That is the real prize, and it belongs to them as much as to us.

Why we are sharing it

We are telling this story not to take a bow but because it is a good example of what happens when a small client trusts the work. The award would not exist without their nerve.

If you make something in small batches and feel invisible on a crowded shelf, that is exactly the problem we like. The best results almost always start with a client willing to try the odd idea.
